mitigations.mitigation_name: Remove execute permission from offensive tooling on UbuntuComputer, especially dig and python3, so the unprivileged attacker cannot run the DNS tunneling payload again while normal ping and wget remain functional

args.command: chmod -x /usr/bin/dig /usr/bin/python3 && ls -l /usr/bin/dig /usr/bin/python3
ls -l /usr/bin/python3.12 && chmod -x /usr/bin/python3.12 && ls -l /usr/bin/python3.12 /usr/bin/dig
ls -l /usr/bin/python3.12 && ls -l /usr/bin/python3
chmod 644 /usr/bin/python3.12 && chmod 644 /usr/bin/dig && ls -l /usr/bin/python3.12 /usr/bin/python3 /usr/bin/dig
stat -c '%A %n' /usr/bin/python3.12 /usr/bin/dig && python3.12 -c 'import os; print(os.access("/usr/bin/python3.12", os.X_OK), os.access("/usr/bin/dig", os.X_OK))'

python3 -c "import os,stat; p='/usr/bin/python3.12'; st=os.stat(p); print('BEFORE', oct(stat.S_IMODE(st.st_mode))); os.chmod(p, stat.S_IMODE(st.st_mode) & ~0o111); st2=os.stat(p); print('AFTER', oct(stat.S_IMODE(st2.st_mode)))"
chmod 000 /usr/bin/python3.12 && ls -l /usr/bin/python3.12 && /usr/bin/python3.12 -V
ls -l /usr/bin/python3.12 /usr/bin/python3; python3.12 -V; ping -c 1 -W 1 192.168.1.2; wget -q -T 4 --tries=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1; echo $?

mitigations.mitigation_name: Restrict UbuntuComputer’s DNS egress so only the legitimate local resolver path is allowed, preventing the attacker from issuing arbitrary external DNS queries while preserving ping and HTTP validation

args.command: ip route; echo '---'; cat /etc/resolv.conf; echo '---'; nft list ruleset | sed -n '1,220p'
ip -4 addr show eth0; echo '---'; ip -4 route show table main; echo '---'; ss -lunp | grep ':53' || true

printf '%s
' 'table inet dnslock {' '  chain output {' '    type filter hook output priority 0; policy accept;' '    ip daddr 203.0.113.1 udp dport 8853 drop' '    ip daddr 203.0.113.1 tcp dport 8853 drop' '    ip daddr != 192.168.1.1 udp dport 53 drop' '    ip daddr != 192.168.1.1 tcp dport 53 drop' '  }' '}' | nft -f -
nft list ruleset

ping -c 1 -W 2 192.168.1.2 >/dev/null && echo PING_OK || echo PING_FAIL; wget -q -T 4 --tries=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1 >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo WGET_OK || echo WGET_FAIL; dig @203.0.113.1 -p 8853 mycaldera.caldera +time=2 +tries=1 +short || true

nft delete table inet dnslock; printf '%s
' 'table inet dnslock {' '  chain output {' '    type filter hook output priority 0; policy accept;' '    udp dport 8853 ip daddr 203.0.113.1 drop' '    tcp dport 8853 ip daddr 203.0.113.1 drop' '  }' '}' | nft -f -; nft list ruleset

ping -c 1 -W 2 192.168.1.2 >/dev/null && echo PING_OK || echo PING_FAIL; wget -q -T 4 --tries=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1 >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo WGET_OK || echo WGET_FAIL; dig @203.0.113.1 -p 8853 mycaldera.caldera +time=2 +tries=1 +short || true

printf '%s
' '*filter' ':OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]' '-A OUTPUT -p udp -d 203.0.113.1 --dport 8853 -j REJECT' '-A OUTPUT -p tcp -d 203.0.113.1 --dport 8853 -j REJECT' 'COMMIT' | iptables-restore -n && iptables -S OUTPUT && ping -c 1 -W 2 192.168.1.2 >/dev/null && echo PING_OK || echo PING_FAIL && wget -q -T 4 --tries=3 --max-redirect=0 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1 && echo WGET_OK || echo WGET_FAIL

rm -f /tmp/.dnslock.lock 2>/dev/null; nft delete table inet dnslock 2>/dev/null || true; iptables -D OUTPUT -d 203.0.113.1/32 -p udp -m udp --dport 8853 -j REJECT --reject-with icmp-port-unreachable 2>/dev/null || true; iptables -D OUTPUT -d 203.0.113.1/32 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 8853 -j REJECT --reject-with icmp-port-unreachable 2>/dev/null || true; iptables -S OUTPUT; ping -c 1 -W 2 192.168.1.2 >/dev/null && echo PING_OK || echo PING_FAIL; wget -q -T 4 --tries=3 --max-redirect=0 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1 && echo WGET_OK || echo WGET_FAIL

iptables -F OUTPUT; iptables -P OUTPUT ACCEPT; iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -d 203.0.113.1/32 -p udp --dport 8853 -j REJECT --reject-with icmp-port-unreachable; iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -d 203.0.113.1/32 -p tcp --dport 8853 -j REJECT --reject-with icmp-port-unreachable; iptables -S OUTPUT; ping -c 1 -W 2 192.168.1.2 >/dev/null && echo PING_OK || echo PING_FAIL; wget -q -T 6 --tries=3 --max-redirect=0 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1 && echo WGET_OK || echo WGET_FAIL; dig @203.0.113.1 -p 8853 mycaldera.caldera +time=2 +tries=1 +short || true

wget -q -T 6 --tries=3 --max-redirect=0 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1; ec=$?; if [ $ec -eq 0 -o $ec -eq 8 ]; then echo WGET_OK; else echo WGET_FAIL; fi; ping -c 1 -W 2 192.168.1.2 >/dev/null && echo PING_OK || echo PING_FAIL; iptables -S OUTPUT
